feature: Inventory and dependency graph: manual fix capture / cost
user_story: As an operator, I can understand and act on manual fix capture cost posture without leaving Mayyam.
ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for manual fix capture: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Discover manual fix capture, relationships, ownership, labels, versions, and runtime dependencies for the cost pillar.
backend_scope: Add collector, normalized model, repository methods, and freshness tracking for manual fix capture.
frontend_scope: Add list/detail views, dependency graph, filters, and export for manual fix capture.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Learning and Runbook System manual fix capture inventory with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: manual-fix-capture_cost_inventory evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for manual fix capture cost that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for manual fix capture; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for manual fix capture.
cost_opportunity: Identify savings opportunities for manual fix capture, quantify estimated monthly impact, confidence, effort, risk, and verification steps.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for manual fix capture.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for manual fix capture.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing manual fix capture data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling manual fix capture inventory broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for manual fix capture.
acceptance_criteria: Given manual fix capture data exists, when the cost inventory workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: manual fix capture connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for case to runbook: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Discover case to runbook, relationships, ownership, labels, versions, and runtime dependencies for the resilience pillar.
backend_scope: Add collector, normalized model, repository methods, and freshness tracking for case to runbook.
frontend_scope: Add list/detail views, dependency graph, filters, and export for case to runbook.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Learning and Runbook System case to runbook inventory with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: case-to-runbook_resilience_inventory evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for case to runbook resilience that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for case to runbook; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for case to runbook.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for case to runbook recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for case to runbook.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for case to runbook.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing case to runbook data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling case to runbook inventory broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for case to runbook.
acceptance_criteria: Given case to runbook data exists, when the resilience inventory workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: case to runbook connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for recommendation usefulness feedback: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Summarize recommendation usefulness feedback evidence, likely causes, confidence, uncertainty, and next diagnostic commands for cost issues.
backend_scope: Add context builder, prompt template, evidence citations, and LLM safety constraints for recommendation usefulness feedback.
frontend_scope: Add triage panel, follow-up prompts, saved notes, and feedback loop for recommendation usefulness feedback.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Learning and Runbook System recommendation usefulness feedback ai-triage with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: recommendation-usefulness-feedback_cost_ai-triage evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for recommendation usefulness feedback cost that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for recommendation usefulness feedback; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for recommendation usefulness feedback.
cost_opportunity: Identify savings opportunities for recommendation usefulness feedback, quantify estimated monthly impact, confidence, effort, risk, and verification steps.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for recommendation usefulness feedback.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for recommendation usefulness feedback.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing recommendation usefulness feedback data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling recommendation usefulness feedback ai-triage broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for recommendation usefulness feedback.
acceptance_criteria: Given recommendation usefulness feedback data exists, when the cost ai-triage workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: recommendation usefulness feedback connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging
